metadata.responder: Guide-#9 (Claude Code Opus 4.7 (1M context), advisory surface)
metadata.subject: rc1-rc4 confirmations + aug1-aug3 fold-in + r1-r4 STOP-and-report bindings
metadata.constraint: Calibration verdict received via LA-forwarded Read of `temp_for_responses/5.0-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_8_CALIBRATION_VERDICT_FOR_GUIDE_9_ITER_1.xml`. Guide-#8 reviewed a digest only; full inline §A-§H not seen by Guide-#8. Items flagged as "not surfaced in digest" are addressed at rc2 below — most ARE present in inline §H per quoted text.

guide_9_calibration_response.top_line: All 4 rc items ACCEPTED. 3 augmentations (aug1-aug3) FOLDED into upgraded F.4 recommendation (Option B+). 4 risks (r1-r4) ABSORBED as explicit STOP-and-report items in forthcoming EA-9 init pointer. Subagent verification discipline (rc3) bound at three surfaces. §H coverage (rc2) confirmed via verbatim quote. Guide-#8 retire-clearance GRANTED from my side; LA decides final timing.

rc1_audit_posture_augmentations.verdict: ACCEPT all 3 augmentations (aug1-aug3). F.4 recommendation upgraded from plain Option B (~15 min) to **Option B+ (inline reconciliation + interaction-map check), ~25-30 min total**. Better Pareto for Stage 5 MEDIUM risk than Option A (full audit subagent, ~30-45 min).

aug1.action: Item 5.4 `<files_to_audit>` scope EXPANDED. Bind under g9-ea9_n1(a).
aug1.expanded_scope: Original dispatch globs (3): - devplatform/tools/fleet_ops/*.py - devplatform/tools/fleet_observability/*.py - devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/*.py Added per aug1 (5): - devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/*.ps1 (Phase C BUG 1 surface — wake_launcher's $RepoRoot/$PSScriptRoot lived here; verify all PS1s post-fix) - devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/*.xml (registered task XMLs — Stage 4.7 rewrote, but verify no stale BlarAI references survived) - devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/**/*.py (server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py + bridge/__main__.py — multi-consumer surface per w22) - devplatform/tools/gate_stale_cleaner/*.py (currently missing from dispatch; fleet aux tool) - devplatform/docs/scheduled/wake_templates/*.md (Stage 4.7.5 already scanned; verify post-Stage-5-deletion no regressions)
aug1.rationale: Phase C demonstrated that scheduled-tasks/*.ps1 was the critical missed surface. w22 KEY LESSON: ".mcp.json is read by THREE different consumers (VS Code MCP loader, PowerShell ConvertFrom-Json, Python config-loader) with different template-expansion behavior" — same defense-in-depth applies to path-reference auditing across consumer types.

aug2.action: Item 5.4 audit verification at Item 5.10 HALT gate. Bind under g9-ea9_n1(b).
aug2.new_step: EA-9 init pointer Item 5.10 instructions add: "EA-9 reports Item 5.4 audit findings to LA chat: (a) full list of files audited, (b) all paths updated with before→after diff stats, (c) post-update Select-String verification showing zero residual `BlarAI\tools\` references in audited surfaces. LA verifies completeness BEFORE approving the merge block."
aug2.rationale: 5.10 is the LA's last review checkpoint before merge to main. Pre-flight audit (Stage 5.0.v2 checkpoint) cannot prevent execution-time audit-incompleteness — 5.10 HALT is the safety net. This is the architectural complement to aug1's pre-flight scope expansion.

aug3.action: Stage 5.5 / 5.5.v2 delete-pattern verification. Bind under g9-ea9_n1(c).
aug3.pre_flight_steps: EA-9 Item 5.5 + 5.5.v2 Pre-Flight (BEFORE any deletion): (a) Get-ChildItem dry-run enumerating files that match each delete_pattern; emit list to LA chat. STOP-and-report if ANY match `docs\platform_separation\temp_for_responses\` (dn13 audit-artifact preservation violation — would destroy Guide-#7/#8/#9 audit chain). (b) Diff against current docs/ state for any NEW Stage-4-era CLAUDE_*, CO_LEAD_*, DEC*, DOMAIN* files added during Stage 4 work — ensure wildcards capture correctly. Stage 4 added PHASE_B_FINDINGS_PLUS_PHASE_C_OPTIONS.xml + PHASE_C_CLOSURE_SUMMARY.xml under temp_for_responses/, not at docs/ root, so neither matches existing patterns AND neither should be touched (preserved per dn13). (c) docs/F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK.md (BlarAI-side copy) — explicit candidate-removal per A13 forward-bind to Stage 5 (relocated to devplatform/docs/runbooks/ at Stage 3.3 expansion). Add as inline Remove-Item or carve as separate sub-item if not in 5.5 patterns.

rc1_audit_posture_augmentations.combined_recommendation: F.4 upgraded recommendation: **Option B+ (inline reconciliation + aug1-aug3 augmentations)**. ~25-30 min total. Covers ~80% of Option A's compound-drift catching at ~33% time cost premium over plain Option B (~15 min). Best Pareto for Stage 5 MEDIUM risk per Guide-#8 dimension b answer.

rc2_h_brief_coverage_confirmation.verdict: CONFIRMED. Inline §D ("Stage 5 Brief For Next Action") covers all 3 items Guide-#8 flagged as Q2 gaps in the digest. Calibration gap was due to digest-vs-inline mismatch, not actual omission.
rc2_h_brief_coverage_confirmation.verbatim_quote_from_inline_d: "Scope (12 v1 + 4 v2 work items): BlarAI-side platform tool deletion (5.1) + .platform/ creation (5.2) + BlarAI coordination file moves to .platform/ (5.3) + devplatform fleet tool path-reference update for the new .platform/ location (5.4) + **platform-doctrine doc deletion from BlarAI/docs/ (5.5 + 5.5.v2: governance + wake_templates)** + **ownership-decisions table (5.5.5)** + pyproject.toml + .gitignore updates (5.6 + 5.7) + **BlarAI test baseline check (5.8 v2 = ~981/22)** + fleet end-to-end verification (5.9, substitute pending d4) + Item 5.10 commit on chore/platform-extraction + HALT for LA merge approval + Item 5.11 tags on both repos + Item 5.12 STATUS append."
rc2_h_brief_coverage_confirmation.note: Bold-emphasis added in this quote for the 3 specific items Guide-#8 flagged. Original inline §D was single-sentence scope summary form. EA-9 init pointer (forthcoming) will be item-by-item per the dispatch contract — that's the operational artifact, not §D.
rc2_h_brief_coverage_confirmation.action_required: None. Calibration concern resolved by quote.

rc3_subagent_verification_discipline.verdict: ACCEPT. Per A18 + dn14/dn20/dn24, never accept subagent claim about file state without `git diff` cross-check. ABSORBED at three surfaces. Bind under g9-ea9_n1(d).

surface_1.name: Stage 5 close S1/S2/S3 triple-subagent playbook (in EA-9 init pointer)

rc3_subagent_verification_discipline.surface_1: EA-9 init pointer will explicitly state: each subagent's claims must be cross-verified by Guide-#9 BEFORE binding into the closure verdict. - S1 (BlarAI-side technical correctness): independent `Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools -Directory` + `git status --porcelain` + spot-checks of .platform/ contents - S2 (devplatform-side path-reference correctness): independent `git diff` showing actual file contents for any flagged path-reference; `Select-String` re-runs of the audit grep over the aug1 expanded scope - S3 (procedural completeness): independent reads of STATUS.md + git log + tag list + merge state - Convergence required across all 3 subagents AND Guide-#9 independent verification = VERIFIED Stage 5 close
rc3_subagent_verification_discipline.surface_2: EA-9 init pointer will include: "Guide-#9 will spawn 3 verification subagents at Stage 5 close per d6/w14. EA-9 cooperates by providing requested artifacts (file lists, git diff outputs, etc.) but does NOT manage subagent design. Independent disk-level verification by Guide-#9 happens AFTER subagents emit; EA-9's role at close is comprehensive close-report cross-checked by Guide-#9 against actual git diff stats per w11."

surface_3.name: My own working principle for Stage 5

rc3_subagent_verification_discipline.surface_3: Never bind a subagent finding into a Guide verdict without independent disk verification. Per dn14: "Do NOT trust the EA's 'I did X' reports without disk-level verification" — same principle applies to subagent reports per d8/A18 inheritance.

rc4_stop_and_report_for_r1_r4.verdict: ACCEPT. All 4 risks absorbed as explicit STOP-and-report items in EA-9 init pointer. Bind under g9-ea9_n1(e), (f), (g), (h).

r1_5_0_5_archive_completeness.stop_and_report_instructions: Post-archive verification additions (dispatch's Write-Warning if $copied -eq 0 is necessary but not sufficient): - EA-9 reports archive zip path + file count + total size to LA chat after Item 5.0.5 completes - STOP if archive zip doesn't exist (Test-Path failure) OR contains zero files when source dirs have non-zero non-pattern-matching files (suggests pattern miss) OR total size < 1KB (suggests empty zip) - LA reviews archive completeness summary BEFORE allowing Item 5.1 deletion to proceed Conditional: if d4#1 (F.1) resolves to Option C ("skip 5.0.5 due to Phase C log migration"), this STOP-and-report is NULL; document the decline in Stage 5 close STATUS append.

r2_5_3_file_move_atomicity.stop_and_report_instructions: Dispatch's Item 5.3 `foreach` loop guards against destination overwrite via `if (Test-Path $m.To) { throw }` but has NO auto-rollback on partial failure. EA-9 wraps loop in try/catch with explicit error capture per move: - On any single Move-Item failure: STOP, do NOT continue loop - Report which moves succeeded (full paths) and which failed (full paths + error) - LA decides recovery: manual revert moved items back to source paths, OR re-run after fixing root cause - Especially relevant if `if (Test-Path $m.To) { throw }` triggers — that throw should halt the loop, not be silently caught

r3_5_5_5_5_v2_delete_pattern_verificatio.stop_and_report_instructions: Pre-deletion dry-run discipline (overlaps with aug3, distinct hard-STOP disposition): - Get-ChildItem dry-run enumerating files that match each delete_pattern in 5.5 + 5.5.v2; emit complete list to LA chat - HARD STOP if ANY match `docs\platform_separation\temp_for_responses\*` (dn13 audit-artifact preservation violation; would destroy Guide audit chain) - HARD STOP if Get-ChildItem returns 0 matches AND there are NEW docs/CLAUDE_*, CO_LEAD_*, DEC*, DOMAIN* files added post-Stage-3 (suggests pattern miss — verify via diff against Stage 3 close manifest) - LA reviews dry-run output BEFORE Item 5.5 / 5.5.v2 deletion executes - aug3(c) addendum: docs/F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK.md BlarAI-side copy explicit candidate-removal per A13; if not in 5.5 patterns, EA-9 carves as separate Remove-Item with `g9-ea9_n*` ack capturing the carve-out

r4_5_6_5_8_baseline_preservation.stop_and_report_instructions: Item 5.6 pyproject.toml `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` testpaths edit must: - REMOVE refs to: tools/vikunja_mcp, tools/fleet_*, tools/autonomy_budget, tools/gate_stale_cleaner, tools/scheduled-tasks (anything platform-tool-side) - PRESERVE testpaths for: shared/, services/, launcher/, tests/, services/policy_agent/tests/, services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/ (BlarAI runtime) Pre-edit: snapshot pyproject.toml `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` block; emit to LA chat Post-edit: re-emit to LA chat with diff Item 5.8.v2 baseline run AFTER 5.6 edit: - HARD STOP if regression below ~981 passed / 22 skipped (allow ±5 fluctuation for flaky tests, but not ±20+) - Report failure summary; LA decides revert-or-fix

additional_acknowledgments.item: Forward-bind as Stage 6.7.5 Ticket MM (NEW): "06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml Item 5.9 references task name `BlarAI_daily_digest` (underscore form); real Title Case Spaces name per CLAUDE.md + Stage 4 evidence is `Daily Digest`. Moot at Stage 5 since task is disabled per Phase A 2026-04-29; correct in dispatch text post-procedure for posterity." Will surface in Stage 5 close STATUS append per Anomalies / Deviations from Plan section. Stage 6.7.5 backlog grows to 21 tickets (was 20 after LL).

item.topic: Tag-creation discipline (Item 5.11) — explicit enumeration in EA-9 init pointer

additional_acknowledgments.item: EA-9 init pointer Item 5.11 will explicitly enumerate: - BlarAI side: `git tag post-platform-extract -m "BlarAI cleanup complete, devplatform authoritative"` - devplatform side: `git tag platform-extracted -m "devplatform established from BlarAI extraction"` - PRESERVE existing `pre-platform-extract` tag (BlarAI side; per dispatch + RECOVERY.md asset coverage matrix)

item.topic: Compound-drift surface confirmation (response to Guide-#8 dimension b answer)

additional_acknowledgments.item: Aug1-aug3 + r1-r4 cover the compound-drift surface I asked about in cs4. Specifically: - Item 5.4 audit (path-reference completeness) × Item 5.10 commit (LA HALT gate): aug1 + aug2 - Item 5.5/5.5.v2 deletion × docs/ current state: aug3 + r3 - Item 5.6 pyproject (test scope) × Item 5.8 baseline: r4 - Item 5.0.5 archive × Item 5.1 deletion: r1 - Item 5.3 sprints/scheduled/active_tasks/docs_sprints moves: r2 - .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock per w20: in Item 5.7 .gitignore augment scope (no additional augmentation needed; already covered by dispatch + watchpoint) No additional augmentations identified. Option B+ now sufficient to match Option A's compound-drift catching.

g9-ea9_n1 will have at least 8 sub-clauses at EA-9 init pointer emission: - n1(a) Item 5.4 scope expansion (aug1) - n1(b) Item 5.4 verification at Item 5.10 HALT gate (aug2) - n1(c) Stage 5.5/5.5.v2 dry-run discipline (aug3) - n1(d) Subagent verification discipline (rc3 / d8 / A18) - n1(e) Item 5.0.5 archive completeness STOP-and-report (r1) - n1(f) Item 5.3 file-move atomicity STOP-and-report (r2) - n1(g) Item 5.5/5.5.v2 dn13-violation HARD STOP (r3 — distinct from aug3: aug3 is dry-run + LA review; r3 is HARD STOP if pattern matches temp_for_responses/) - n1(h) Item 5.6 × 5.8 baseline preservation STOP-and-report (r4) Pending LA F.1-F.4 resolutions, additional sub-clauses bind: - n1(i) F.1 resolution (Item 5.0.5 scope: A/B/C) - n1(j) F.2 resolution (Item 5.9 substitute: A/B/C/D) - n1(k) F.3 resolution (Tickets II/JJ DEFER vs FOLD) - n1(l) F.4 resolution (audit posture: A/B+/C; B+ recommended post-rc1) Total g9-ea9_n1: 8-12 sub-clauses depending on LA F.1-F.4 dispositions. Cumulative-ack chain at Stage 5 EA-9 spawn will be: 116 (Stage 4 close) + 8-12 (g9-ea9_n1) = **124-128 binding atoms**.

retire_clearance_acknowledgment.my_view: Guide-#8's retire-clearance is **GRANTED FROM MY SIDE**. All rc1-rc4 confirmed. Calibration loop discharged from successor's perspective. Items not_required_for_clearance per Guide-#8 (cs7 w22 forward-only, F.2 doc-defect catch, Item 5.11 tag specifics) all addressed in i1-i4 above.
retire_clearance_acknowledgment.la_decision_pending: LA holds final retire timing. Suggested timeline: LA reviews this RC1-RC4 confirmation response + Guide-#8 acknowledges; Guide-#8 retires after acknowledgment. Estimated Guide-#9 follow-up turn count to retire-clearance: this turn. Per Guide-#8 retire_timeline: "~5-10 min real time."

guide_9_calibration_response.next_action_for_la: One LA action outstanding before Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer drafting: (1) **Resolve F.1-F.4** (4 scope decisions surfaced in Guide-#9 iter-1 §F): 5.0.5 archive scope (A/B/C) / 5.9 verification substitute (A/B/C/D) / II+JJ defer-vs-fold / 5.0.v2 audit posture (A/B+/C — Option B+ recommended post-rc1). After (1), I draft the Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer per handoff `<first_action_for_successor>` step 5 + the accumulated g9-ea9_n1 amendments (8-12 sub-clauses depending on F.1-F.4 dispositions) + the established 4.0-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_8_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml pattern.
